advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Distinguish setup-error/no-run from clean skip in the canary (e.g. also inspect setup-phase failed reports) or reword the FAIL message so a setup-error run is not reported as 'every live-Jira test skipped'.
evidence: tests/external/conftest.py:75: `if report.when == "call" and not report.skipped:` — only call-phase, non-skipped reports mark a nodeid executed
tests/external/conftest.py:96-97: `executed = sum(1 for it in jira_items if it.nodeid in executed_nodeids)` then `skipped = collected - executed` — a setup-error test counts toward `skipped`
tests/external/conftest.py:101-103: the printed FAIL message asserts 'every live-Jira test skipped' which is inaccurate when tests errored in setup
advisory.finding: `pytest_runtest_logreport` records a test as executed only for `report.when == 'call' and not report.skipped`, so a jira_live test that ERRORS during setup (e.g. a fixture failing because live Jira auth broke) emits no call-phase report and is counted as 'not executed' — the same bucket as a clean skip. The canary then fires 'every live-Jira test skipped' even though a test actually attempted to run and failed at setup, mislabeling the failure.
scenarios: A jira_live test whose fixture raises (broken auth, acli present but unauthenticated) rather than cleanly skipping

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Make ACLI_SHA256 verification mandatory (fail, not warn) so no acli binary is installed without a pinned digest.
evidence:
"if [ -n "${ACLI_SHA256:-}" ]; then
 echo "${ACLI_SHA256}  $HOME/.acli/acli.tar.gz" | sha256sum -c --strict
 else
 echo "::warning::ACLI_SHA256 unset — proceeding without checksum verification."
The binary is then symlinked into /usr/local/bin and fed a Jira API token: 'echo "$JIRA_API_TOKEN" | acli jira auth login'
"- name: Download + verify + install acli\n if: steps.cache-acli.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'"
Cache key: "key: acli-${{ runner.os }}-${{ vars.ACLI_VERSION || 'unset' }}" — keyed only on version, not on the SHA256 digest.
advisory.finding: The acli binary is downloaded over the network and installed to PATH with credentials attached, but checksum verification is optional: when ACLI_SHA256 is unset the workflow prints a warning and proceeds without verifying provenance.
merged_from: On a cache hit the entire download/verify/install step is skipped (`if: steps.cache-acli.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'`), so a cached acli binary is never re-verified against ACLI_SHA256 before being placed on PATH and given credentials.
advisory.suggested_fix: Change the else branch to `echo "::error::ACLI_SHA256 unset"; exit 1` so the workflow refuses to install an unverified binary.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test that drives `pytest_sessionfinish` with a stubbed session (jira_live items + populated executed set) under REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL and asserts `session.exitstatus` flips to TESTS_FAILED on all-skip and stays clean when at least one executed.
evidence: tests/external/conftest.py:94-105 `jira_items = [it for it in session.items if it.get_closest_marker("jira_live") is not None]` ... `executed = sum(1 for it in jira_items if it.nodeid in executed_nodeids)` ... `session.exitstatus = pytest.ExitCode.TESTS_FAILED`

tests/unit/test_external_canary.py only calls `_conftest._jira_canary_should_fail(...)` and `_conftest.pytest_collection_modifyitems(...)`; no test invokes `pytest_sessionfinish` or asserts an exitstatus flip (ABSENCE)
A regression in the marker/nodeid counting or the exitstatus assignment would let a scheduled all-skip run stay green — the exact failure this change exists to prevent — and no test would catch it.
advisory.finding: The end-to-end fail-on-all-skip behavior in `pytest_sessionfinish` is untested; tests only cover the extracted pure `_jira_canary_should_fail` decision, leaving the session-integration glue (mapping `session.items` -> collected/executed counts and flipping `session.exitstatus` to `pytest.ExitCode.TESTS_FAILED`) with no regression coverage.
scenarios: get_closest_marker / marker name changes and jira_live tests stop being counted
exitstatus flip line is removed or gated on the wrong condition

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test feeding `pytest_runtest_logreport` fake reports (call+passed, call+skipped, setup+skipped) and assert only the executed call-phase nodeid is recorded in `_executed_set`.
evidence: No test in tests/unit/test_external_canary.py constructs a TestReport (call/setup, skipped/not) and asserts which nodeids land in the executed set (ABSENCE)

If this predicate regressed to count setup-phase reports or skipped-call reports as executed, the canary would treat skipped tests as executed and never fail — defeating the guard — with no test catching it.
advisory.finding: `pytest_runtest_logreport`'s executed-vs-skipped detection (`report.when == 'call' and not report.skipped`) is untested, so the mechanism that distinguishes a test that ran from one that skipped has no regression coverage.
scenarios: the `not report.skipped` guard is dropped so skipped calls count as executed

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Make ACLI_SHA256 verification mandatory (hard-fail when unset) in the acli download step instead of falling through to a warning-and-proceed.
evidence: `if [ -n "${ACLI_SHA256:-}" ]; then` ... `else` ... `echo "::warning::ACLI_SHA256 unset — proceeding without checksum verification.`

Downstream the unverified binary is executed and given the secret: line 100 `"$HOME/.acli/acli" --version`, line 112-116 `sudo ln -sf "$HOME/.acli/acli" /usr/local/bin/acli` then `echo "$JIRA_API_TOKEN" | acli jira auth login`

Reachability is bounded: the workflow triggers are only `schedule` (cron 0 6 * * 1) and `workflow_dispatch` (lines 17-24) with `permissions: contents: read` (line 26-27) — not fork-triggerable; and this step is a verbatim mirror of the pre-existing, unchanged reconcile-bridge.yml (lines 143-172), so it is an inherited pattern rather than newly-authored logic.
advisory.finding: The acli download step verifies the tarball checksum only when ACLI_SHA256 is set; when the operator leaves it unset the step emits a `::warning::` and proceeds to extract, chmod +x, root-symlink, and execute the downloaded binary (which is then handed the JIRA_API_TOKEN) with NO integrity verification — a fail-open on supply-chain integrity.
scenarios: Operator sets ACLI_VERSION but forgets ACLI_SHA256; a compromised/MITM'd acli.atlassian.com response is installed and executed with the Jira token on the scheduled runner without any integrity gate.
advisory.suggested_fix: Replace the `else`/`::warning::` branch with a hard failure: `else echo "::error::ACLI_SHA256 repo variable is required for checksum verification"; exit 1; fi`, mirroring the ACLI_VERSION guard above it.

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the fail-on-all-skip session glue in pytest_sessionfinish.
coaching.subject: the fail-on-all-skip session glue in pytest_sessionfinish

coaching.coaching: Threat-model the change to the optional acli checksum verification path (trust boundary, input validation).
coaching.subject: the optional acli checksum verification path
